title: Technobeast
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Mutant|mutated]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborgs]]|the [[junk golem]]s of [[Kazdan Paratus]] or the [[Trailian technobeast]]s of [[Cronal]]}}

{{Quote|Maggot of metal, rust, and rot. Sith life draws breath, old life does not.|Sith incantation of ''mechu-deru vitae'', a technique used to create technobeasts|<ref name="NEGD" />}}
'''Technobeasts''' were [[Cyborg/Legends|part machine, part organic]] [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] [[mutant]]s. They were created by infecting living organisms with the [[nanogene spore]], a [[Nanovirus|technovirus]] developed through a combination of [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]] and a [[Force power/Legends|Force technique]] called ''[[mechu-deru]]''. The virus grew metallic tumors over the bodies of its victims, ultimately [[Lobotomy|lobotomizing]] their [[Brain/Legends|brains]] and transforming them into weaponized cyborgs. Living subjects from a number of different [[Species/Legends|species]] were infected by the virus, spawning mutated amalgamations of metal and flesh in a variety of shapes and sizes.

In the last quarter of the [[New Sith Wars]], a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] known as [[Belia Darzu]] devised a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] ritual called ''[[mechu-deru vitae]]'', using it to build her own [[Army/Legends|army]] of technobeasts—the [[Metanecrons]]. Housing the creatures inside a hidden [[Belia Darzu's fortress|stronghold]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Darzu used her army of mutated cyborgs against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] during the two-[[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] [[Sictis Wars]]. The subsequent [[Assassination of Belia Darzu|assassination]] of Darzu ended the creation of new Sithspawn hybrids, leading to their disuse after the [[Ruusan campaign|conclusion]] of the New Sith Wars.

==Characteristics==
===Transformation===
[[File:TechBeastSchematic.jpg|thumb|left|225px|Schematic of a humanoid technobeast. {{C|1. lobotomized brain 2. nanospore pores 3. saw blade 4. cutting blade 5. metallic tumor 6. metallic growths}}]]

Technobeasts were characterized by an infection from the [[nanogene spore]], a [[Nanovirus|technovirus]] capable of transmuting living tissue into metallic parts.<ref name="NEGD" /> Consisting of microscopic [[nanogene droid]]s,<ref name="END" /> the technovirus instinctively moved towards the [[Brain/Legends|brain]] of the victim, [[Lobotomy|lobotomizing]] their frontal lobe and rendering them incapable of higher thought. By this point, the subject's transformation into a [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] hybrid was irreversible. Before the lobotomy took place, however, it was possible to halt the transformation through the power of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]],<ref name="NEGD" /> or by purging the nanogene [[Droid/Legends|droids]] with intense heat.<ref name="DBROT" /> While they were initially deemed intelligent beings,<ref name="CSWE-TECH">{{CSWECite|III|227|Technobeast}}</ref> technobeasts with long-term exposure to the nanogene spore would undergo severe mental degradation, limiting their motor functions and reducing the creatures to clumsy, shambling automata.<ref name="DBROT" />

Once fully transformed, most remnants of living tissue on the creatures would have rotted away, leaving only small strands of skin and sinew surrounded by metallic scraps, rods and wires. The transformation also deteriorated the victim's vocal cords, rendering the technobeasts incapable of speech.<ref name="DBROT" /> Due to the innately experimental nature of the nanogene spore, the manner in which the subjects were transformed had varying results, making all technobeasts unique. Some of the creatures possessed zigzags of scarred tissue caused by the virus building upon its own metallic chunks. In other cases, victims ended up with multiple heads or [[Crab/Legends|crab]]-like pincers.<ref name="NEGD" />

===Combat capabilities===
Designed primarily for destruction, most humanoid technobeasts had their arms replaced with skewers or saws,<ref name="NEGD" /> while others were known to have jagged blades extending from their elbows. Larger technobeast variants—specifically those created from [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]] and [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]]—were forged into war machines. In particular, the [[Mutant|mutated]] rancors brandished a very large [[Claw/Legends|claw]] that resembled a [[Club/Legends|club]], though they were also known to bite their foes when given the opportunity. Along with the transformed banthas, the rancors bore spiked [[Armor/Legends|armor]] plating to replace their hides, in addition to having [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]] fused to their shoulders.<ref name="DBROT" />

Infested with the nanogene spore, technobeasts were able to infect other living organisms with the virus, potentially transforming additional victims into the creatures themselves.<ref name="END" /> The [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] variants were capable of dispersing clouds of the technovirus from their [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]]. In combat, the creatures became enraged while attacking their foes, relentlessly swarming against them in large numbers. Despite their aggressive nature, the technobeasts attacked in a slow, cumbersome manner. In addition, the creatures were vulnerable to heavy [[Telekinesis/Legends|blasts]] of energy from the Force, as well as [[Force lightning/Legends|Sith lightning]] and [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] strikes.<ref name="DBROT" />

==History==
===Origins===
{{Quote|It is believed that through their bizarre alchemies, the Sith somehow stumbled upon the ability to manipulate mechanicals of complex nature through the Force: ships, computers, and, indeed, even droids.|Tam Azur-Jamin|<ref name="DTATF">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}}</ref>}}
[[Millennium/Legends|Thousands]] of years before the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], a small number of [[Sith sorcerer|Sith magicians]] discovered ''[[mechu-deru]]'', a [[Force power/Legends|Force technique]] granting them the ability to manipulate mechanical structures. Using this technique, the Sith created [[Cyborg/Legends|part-machine, part-organic]] hybrids known as technobeasts. The power of ''mechu-deru'' was practiced by only a few at this time, causing its secrets to fade into obscurity.<ref name="TOTJC" /><ref name="EN-DATF">{{Blog|abelgpena|47|Endnotes for ''Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena, Part I''}}</ref>

===Resurgence===
{{Quote|This power, known as mechu-deru, was responsible for the creation of Belia Darzu's infamous Technobeasts, which were capable of rewriting an organism's entire genetic code.|Tam Azur-Jamin|<ref name="DTATF" />}}
[[File:BeliaDarzuCropped.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Belia Darzu, a Sith alchemist who created technobeasts during the New Sith Wars]]

In a later period called the [[New Sith Wars]],<ref name="NEGD" /> a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] by the name of [[Belia Darzu]] researched ''mechu-deru'' alongside [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]].<ref name="DBROT" /> With her knowledge of both, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Darzu derived a new technique known as ''[[mechu-deru vitae]]'', using it to transform living beings into technobeasts by means of a nanogene virus.<ref name="NEGD" /> Bound to Darzu's will and imbued with the power of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]],<ref name="DBROT" /><ref name="CSWE-DARZU">{{CSWECite|I|172|Darzu, Belia}}</ref> the technobeasts were created and housed inside a secret [[Belia Darzu's fortress|fortress]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tython/Legends|Tython]]. Within this stronghold, living beings from several different humanoid and [[Alien/Legends|non-humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|races]] were transformed into technobeasts themselves,<ref name="DBROT" /> forming an [[Army/Legends|army]] of the creatures known as the [[Metanecrons]].<ref name="END" /> In addition, Darzu constructed a [[Holocron of Belia Darzu|holocron]] containing her knowledge of the techniques used to create the Sithspawn creatures.<ref name="DBROT" />

The technobeasts of Belia Darzu were used against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> becoming recognized as the signature horror of a two-[[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] conflict known as the [[Sictis Wars]].<ref name="NEGD" /> In combat, the mutated hybrids infected other living beings with the nanogene virus, assimilating Darzu's fallen enemies into her cyborg army.<ref name="END" /> On one occasion, however, a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] prevented the virus from fully transforming him into a Sithspawn mutant, preserving his identity and becoming known as the "[[technobeast Jedi]]."<ref name="CSWE-TECH" /> By the end of the Sictis Wars in [[1230 BBY]],<ref name="NEGD" /> the production of new technobeasts came to a halt after Darzu was [[Assassination of Belia Darzu|assassinated]],<ref name="DBROT" /><ref name="NEGD" /> sabotaging her army.<ref name="END" /> Despite this, subsequent Sith Lords continued to utilize the creatures, though in smaller numbers than before. The use of technobeasts ended when the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were defeated at the [[Ruusan campaign]] in [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].<ref name="NEGD" /><ref name="TEA" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I need a new Master—one with the power to penetrate the defenses of Belia's stronghold and lay claim to her secrets.|Hetton|<ref name="DBROT" />}}
[[File:DarthBane-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|200px|Protected by his orbalisk armor, Darth Bane destroyed a horde of technobeasts on the planet Tython.]]

Over two [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] after the fall of Lord Darzu, some of her technobeasts remained active inside her [[Fortress/Legends|stronghold]] on Tython. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] of disuse had left them in a dilapidated, rusted state, causing some of their weapons to malfunction. In addition, the creatures became mentally and physically debilitated by the long-term effects of the nanogene spore. Drawn towards the dark side energy radiating from the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] of Belia Darzu, the surviving technobeasts congregated around the device within the inner sanctum of the Tython fortress.<ref name="DBROT" />

In [[990 BBY]], a Sith Lord known as [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] encountered the technobeasts on Tython while [[Mission to Tython|seeking]] the holocron of Belia Darzu. When Bane attempted to seize the device, the horde of mutated cyborgs immediately attacked him in waves. Despite their efforts to kill the Sith Lord, the creatures were unable to penetrate the defenses of his [[orbalisk armor]]. At various points throughout the skirmish, the technobeasts infected Bane with the nanogene spore, spewing clouds of the virus in his vicinity. Nonetheless, the [[orbalisk]] parasites on Bane's armor were able to purge the virus from his body, preventing the transformation. Despite their persistent attacks, the technobeasts inside the fortress were ultimately destroyed at the hands of Bane.<ref name="DBROT" />

Centuries later, a number of [[Sith scroll/Legends|Sith scrolls]] containing Belia Darzu's knowledge of ''mechu-deru'' were acquired by [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren]], an agent of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="PAWNS">{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}}</ref><ref name="END" /> In addition, the son of Roganda—the cyborg known as [[Irek Ismaren]]—possessed an accelerated mastery of ''mechu-deru'' by means of a brain transplant. Together, the pair had the potential to create and unleash a new horde of technobeasts upon the inhabitants of the galaxy.<ref name="PAWNS" /> During the era of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the technobeasts and methods used to create them were discussed in [[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|''Droids, Technology and the Force'']], an essay written by Jedi Knight [[Tam Azur-Jamin]].<ref name="DTATF" />

==Behind the scenes==
The technobeasts were first mentioned unnamed in ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'', a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] supplement published by [[West End Games]] in [[1996]].<ref name="TOTJC" /> The creatures received yet another unidentified reference in [[The Emperor's Pawns|''The Lost Art'']], an adventure seed featured in the [[2001]] article entitled ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]''.<ref name="PAWNS" /> In [[2005]], both of these references were [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] by [[Abel G. Peña]], an author who named the creatures in a [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace fan club]] article called [[Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)|''Droids, Technology and the Force'']].<ref name="EN-DATF" /> ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' provided more detailed information regarding the technobeasts in [[2006]], along with the first images of the creatures, by artist [[Ian Fullwood]].<ref name="NEGD" /> The technobeasts made their debut appearance in ''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'', the second novel of [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s [[Star Wars: Darth Bane Trilogy|''Darth Bane Trilogy'']].<ref name="DBROT" /> The [[Junk golem|Junk Golem]] figurine included in [[The Force Unleashed (Star Wars Miniatures)|''The Force Unleashed'']] [[Star Wars Miniatures|miniatures]] set was once called a "technobeast."<ref name="SWG-TFU">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20080222.html|text=Play ''The Force Unleashed'' Characters on your Tabletop}}</ref> In the third part of [[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]], the alchemical creations of a character named [[Cronal]] were also dubbed "[[Trailian technobeast]]s."<ref name="DOAE-3">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-3|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 3|nolive=1}}</ref>
